---
title: "Counting Inversions HTML Encoding"
output: github_document
---

# HTML Encoding Map

The Counting Inversions page is encoded as one algorithm card with these sections:

1. `\<title>` and the visible heading label the page as "Counting Inversions".
2. The top bar holds the navigation buttons and the asymptotic complexity boxes.
3. `Problem Description` defines an inversion as a pair `i < j` with `S[i] > S[j]`.
4. `Code` uses the shared `codeBox` container and pulls the source from `main.js`.
5. `Algorithm Steps` is stored as a `\<ul>` whose items all follow the `\<li><p>...\</p></li>` pattern.
6. `Explanation` describes the merge-sort-based divide-and-conquer strategy and how cross inversions are counted during merging.
7. `Example/Demo` is an output `\<pre>` block for the live console-style example.

# Structural Notes

The language across the page is aligned to the actual JavaScript implementation: recursive splitting, merge-based counting, `O(n log n)` runtime, and `O(n)` extra space. The page also keeps the standard `data-source` and `data-runtime` attributes for the viewer.
